branch: MORI/AITER hot-helper W13+W2 fused hot-weight collective

code_change.summary: Add an opt-in autograd Function that packs hot W13 and W2 rows into one flat buffer, all-reduces once in forward/backward, then splits owner gradients back to local W13 and W2.

attempts.change: Initial fused W13/W2 collective returned strided split views.
Return contiguous W13/W2 hot tensors after splitting the fused flat buffer.
attempts.outcome: Still rejected; contiguity did not remove the catastrophic first-microbatch cost.

comparison.decision: Do not promote fused W13/W2 hot-weight collective. Keep the separate W13 and W2 hot-weight collectives for the MORI/AITER route-mask branch.

diagnosis.what_was_tested: Whether combining W13 and W2 hot-weight broadcast/all-reduce into one symmetric all-rank collective would reduce helper partial reduction overhead.
diagnosis.what_failed: The monolithic fused hot-weight collective makes each fwd/bwd microbatch tens of seconds, even after returning contiguous split tensors.
diagnosis.likely_reason: The fused slab is too large and poorly scheduled for the current RCCL/MORI/AITER execution path; it removes two smaller collectives but creates one much larger blocking collective/copy point that destroys helper-path overlap and progress.
diagnosis.useful_lesson: The helper dW partial reduction is not missing. Owner scatter is small; the remaining cost is collective wait/scheduling around hot weight broadcast/reduction, route planning/masks, and helper compute placement.

next_actions: Keep W13 and W2 separate and profile the current route-mask W13-fixed branch with hot-weight timing plus rank-max stage aggregation.
Avoid rank-divergent skips; all ranks must enter the same hot-weight collective graph.
Investigate owner-targeted MORI broadcast/reduce or chunked/pipelined hot-weight transfer instead of one dense all-reduce slab.
Compare the stage-level MORI/AITER trace against Ascend 8-layer and 12-layer force-balance traces for all-to-all, all-gather, reduce-scatter, and MoE helper execution.

validation.remote_materialization: failed/rejected before a completed step because first microbatch latency was already non-promotable
